# Brinjal, Chilli, Cauliflower, Okra
# Generated by KrishiMitra AI Knowledge Base Builder
# Source: ICAR guidelines + agricultural research literature
# Last updated: 2024-25 season

# ICAR Package of Practices for Vegetable Crops in India

## Introduction
This document provides detailed guidelines for the cultivation of brinjal (Solanum melongena), chilli (Capsicum annuum), cauliflower (Brassica oleracea botrytis), and okra/bhindi (Abelmoschus esculentus) based on recommendations from ICAR, CIB&RC, and other relevant agricultural research institutions. The guidelines include specific variety names, fertilization schedules, pest management strategies, disease control measures, irrigation timings, and the expected market support prices for 2024-25.

---

## Brinjal (Solanum melongena)

### Varieties
- Pusa Purple Long
- Arka Nidhi
- Hybrids

### Planting Details
- Spacing: 60 cm x 45 cm

### Nutrient Management
- Fertilizer Application:
  - Nitrogen (N): 150 kg/ha
  - Phosphorus (P): 75 kg/ha
  - Potassium (K): 75 kg/ha
  - Boron: 2.5 kg/ha

### Irrigation Management
- Critical Irrigation Timings:
  - First irrigation after sowing
  - Second and third irrigations at 10-14 days interval post-sowing
  - Subsequent irrigation every 7-10 days depending on soil moisture content

### Pest Management
- Shoot and Fruit Borer ETL: 5% damage control
  - Chlorpyrifos 20EC: 2 mL/L or Spinosad
- Aphids and Whitefly Control:
  - Imidacloprid (17.8SL): 0.3 mL/L

### Disease Management
- Phomopsis blight control:
  - Mancozeb 2.5 g/L

### Yield Expectation
- 200-300 quintals/ha

---

## Chilli (Capsicum annuum)

### Varieties
- Pusa Jwala
- LCA-235
- Hybrids

### Planting Details
- Spacing: 60 cm x 45 cm

### Nutrient Management
- Fertilizer Application:
  - Nitrogen (N): 120 kg/ha
  - Phosphorus (P): 60 kg/ha
  - Potassium (K): 60 kg/ha

### Irrigation Management
- Critical Irrigation Timings:
  - First irrigation after sowing
  - Second and third irrigations at 10-14 days interval post-sowing
  - Subsequent irrigation every 7-10 days depending on soil moisture content

### Pest Management
- Thrips Control:
  - Spinosad: 0.3 mL/L
- Mites Control:
  - Abamectin
- Fruit Borer Control:
  - Emamectin benzoate

### Disease Management
- Anthracnose Colletotrichum control:
  - Copper oxychloride + Mancozeb
- Powdery mildew control:
  - Sulphur: 3 g/L
- Leaf curl virus control (whitefly):
  - Use resistant varieties

### Yield Expectation
- Green chilli: 80-120 quintals/ha
- Dry chilli: 15-20 quintals/ha

---

## Cauliflower (Brassica oleracea botrytis)

### Varieties
- Pusa Snowball K-1
- Pusa Sharad

### Planting Details
- Spacing: 60 cm x 45 cm

### Nutrient Management
- Fertilizer Application:
  - Nitrogen (N): 120 kg/ha
  - Phosphorus (P): 60 kg/ha
  - Potassium (K): 60 kg/ha
  - Boron: 1 kg/ha

### Irrigation Management
- Critical Irrigation Timings:
  - First irrigation after sowing
  - Second and third irrigations at 10-14 days interval post-sowing
  - Subsequent irrigation every 7-10 days depending on soil moisture content

### Pest Management
- Diamond-back moth control:
  - Bt: 2 kg/ha or Spinosad

### Disease Management
- Black rot control:
  - Copper compounds

### Yield Expectation
- 200-300 quintals/ha

---

## Okra/Bhindi (Abelmoschus esculentus)

### Varieties
- Pusa A-4
- Arka Anamika
- VRO-6

### Planting Details
- Spacing: 45 cm x 30 cm

### Nutrient Management
- Fertilizer Application:
  - Nitrogen (N): 100 kg/ha
  - Phosphorus (P): 60 kg/ha
  - Potassium (K): 60 kg/ha

### Irrigation Management
- Critical Irrigation Timings:
  - First irrigation after sowing
  - Second and third irrigations at 10-14 days interval post-sowing
  - Subsequent irrigation every 7-10 days depending on soil moisture content

### Pest Management
- Jassids and Whitefly (YVMV vector) control:
  - Imidacloprid (17.8SL): 0.3 mL/L
- Fruit borer control:
  - Profenofos + Cypermethrin

### Disease Management
- Yellow Vein Mosaic Virus control (whitefly):
  - Use resistant varieties

### Yield Expectation
- 80-100 quintals/ha

---

## Market Support Prices for 2024-25
- Brinjal: MSP not fixed, market price expected to be around Rs. 300-350 per kg
- Chilli (green): MSP not fixed, market price expected to be around Rs. 180-200 per kg
- Cauliflower: MSP not fixed, market price expected to be around Rs. 400-450 per kg
- Okra/Bhindi: MSP not fixed, market price expected to be around Rs. 60-70 per kg

---

## Conclusion
These guidelines are designed to help farmers achieve optimal yields and manage pests and diseases effectively while adhering to the recommended practices by ICAR and CIB&RC. Farmers should regularly monitor their crops for signs of pest infestations and disease outbreaks, and adjust management strategies as necessary.

For further assistance or detailed information, contact your local agricultural extension office or visit the nearest ICAR-Central Institute of Indian Agriculture Research (CIIAR) center.
